madrat 3.36.2

    • Portable unagreggated collections (PUCs) in MADRaT

cachePut

Source: R/cachePut.R
cachePut.Rd

Save data to cache

Usage

cachePut(x, prefix, type, fname, callString)

Arguments

x

data that should be written to cache

prefix

function prefix (e.g. "calc" or "read")

type

output type (e.g. "TauTotal")

fname

The name of the cache file to be written.

callString

A string representation of the function call that leads to the cache file being written. Will be attached as an attribute.

See also

cachePut, cacheName

Author

Jan Philipp Dietrich, Pascal Sauer

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
fname <- madrat:::cacheName("calc", "Example")
madrat:::cachePut(1, "calc", "Example", fname, 'calcOutput("Example")')
} # }

On this page

Developed by Jan Philipp Dietrich, Pascal Sauer, Lavinia Baumstark, Stephen Wirth, Anastasis Giannousakis, Renato Rodrigues, Benjamin Leon Bodirsky, Debbora Leip, Michael Crawford, Ulrich Kreidenweis, David Klein, Patrick Rein.
